reforms.description: Belgium aims to streamline public support for R&D and innovation across regions to enhance the innovative capacity of firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the efficiency of public support, the reform is expected to enhance firm-level innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform aims to better streamline public support for R&D and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to enhance the effectiveness of existing support mechanisms for innovation, which is crucial for improving overall economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: Further streamline public support for R&D and innovation within each region.

reforms.description: Belgium plans to reduce administrative burdens on SMEs to foster entrepreneurship and improve business dynamism.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing administrative burdens is expected to lower barriers to entry for new firms, thereby enhancing entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Start-ups face several barriers and entrepreneurship is underdeveloped.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses significant barriers to entrepreneurship, which is vital for enhancing the overall business environment and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: Further reduce administrative burdens on SMEs stemming from federal and regional measures.

reforms.description: The regional governments in Belgium have established significant investment plans aimed at rehabilitating transport infrastructure, including a EUR640 million plan in Wallonia and a EUR3.6 billion plan in Flanders for the Oosterweel Junction.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investments in transport infrastructure are expected to enhance connectivity, reduce congestion, and improve overall economic efficiency, thereby supporting long-term growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Investment plans have been established and are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: These investment plans represent a substantial commitment to improving critical infrastructure, which is essential for economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government of Wallonia has set up a EUR640 million investment plan for the period 2016-19 to rehabilitate inland waterways and highways.

reforms.description: The Federal government proposed to establish a Strategic Investment Pact aimed at mobilizing public and private funds for public investment, with a focus on supporting innovative firms and funding for research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Mobilizing funds for innovative firms and R&D can enhance productivity and foster economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The final strategy note outlining the government’s intentions will be presented in early 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a Strategic Investment Pact represents a significant structural change in how public and private investments are coordinated, particularly in the context of innovation and R&D funding.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal government has proposed to establish a Strategic Investment Pact. The aim is to mobilize public and private funds for public investment, ensure the sustainability of public finances and promote structural reform.

reforms.description: The government introduced a personal income tax reduction for investments in start-ups, providing a tax shelter for direct investments and indirect investments via licensed crowdfunding platforms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging investments in start-ups can stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship, leading to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: As of mid-2015, investors benefit from this tax shelter for direct investments up to 100000 EUR in start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform provides a significant incentive for private investment in start-ups, enhancing the overall startup ecosystem in Belgium.
reforms.source_quote: A new personal income tax reduction from 30% to 45% was introduced for investments in start-ups.

reforms.description: The Federal government has proposed several changes to the Competitiveness Law of 1996 to better safeguard cost competitiveness while maintaining wage indexation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By ensuring wage growth aligns with productivity, the reform aims to enhance competitiveness and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Federal government proposed several changes to the Competitiveness Law.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed changes represent a significant adjustment to existing wage-setting mechanisms, which could have broad implications for competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: As part of the 2017 budget, the Federal government proposed several changes to the Competitiveness Law of 1996.

reforms.description: The federal and regional governments have initiated efforts to simplify administrative procedures for SMEs, including micro-firms, through the Brussels-Capital Region’s Small Business Act.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying administrative procedures can lower barriers to entry for startups and SMEs, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Federal and regional initiatives aimed at simplifying administrative procedures for SMEs have been established.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to enhance the business environment for SMEs, which is crucial for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Federal and regional initiatives aimed at simplifying administrative procedures for SMEs, including for micro-firms.

reforms.description: Belgium aims to improve the business environment for self-employed individuals and SMEs by reducing social security contributions, increasing R&D and innovation budgets, simplifying administrative procedures, and enhancing access to finance and public procurement.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing costs and improving access to resources, the initiative is expected to stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship, thereby enhancing productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was outlined as part of the measures to improve the business environment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant effort to enhance the business environment, which is crucial for fostering innovation and entrepreneurship in Belgium.
reforms.source_quote: Measures to improve the business environment for self-employed and SMEs, especially through a reduction of social security contributions, increased R&D and innovation budgets...

reforms.description: The government is focusing on improving the effectiveness of existing tax exemptions for R&D activities to enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing tax exemptions for R&D is expected to incentivize firms to invest more in research and development, leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is addressing the effectiveness of existing tax exemptions for R&D activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets the improvement of a key instrument for promoting R&D investment among firms.
reforms.source_quote: Key challenges for Belgium include improving the effectiveness of existing tax exemptions for R&D activities...

reforms.description: The federal and regional governments have launched several programs to foster firm-level R&D and innovation, with a focus on public financing of basic research to support breakthrough innovations.
reforms.package_name: Belgium R&D and Innovation Support Programs
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Public financing of R&D is essential for fostering innovation and enhancing productivity, which can lead to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Programs have been launched to support firm-level R&D and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how R&D and innovation are supported at both federal and regional levels, which is crucial for enhancing Belgium's productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The federal and regional governments have launched a number of programs to foster firm-level R&D and innovation.

reforms.description: The tax credits system is being reformed to align with OECD standards, abolishing the patent box regime and introducing a new innovation income deduction regime.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining tax incentives for R&D can enhance investment in innovation, thereby supporting productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The patent box regime was abolished in July 2016, and a new innovation income deduction regime was approved.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major overhaul of the tax incentives system for R&D, aligning it with international standards and potentially increasing R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: The tax credits system is being reformed... with the objective to bring the new regime in line with the OECD G20 minimum standards.

reforms.description: The Flemish authorities have established various R&D funding options, including support for feasibility studies, SME innovation projects, and funding for the development of new innovative products, with specific ceilings for each type of funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases financial support for R&D activities, thereby enhancing innovation capacity among SMEs and fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Flemish authorities have established a number of R&D funding options.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the funding landscape for R&D in the Flemish Region, aimed at enhancing innovation and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The Flemish authorities have established a number of R&D funding options.

reforms.description: The Federal government is implementing measures within its 'SMEs plan' to reduce administrative costs associated with firm registration by 30%, including the creation of a single administrative contact for SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reduces barriers to entry for new firms, facilitating entrepreneurship and potentially increasing economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The Federal government is implementing measures within its 'SMEs plan'.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant in streamlining processes for SMEs, which can enhance their operational efficiency and support growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal government is implementing measures within its 'SMEs plan' to reduce the administrative costs associated with firm registration by 30%.

reforms.description: Belgium is considering establishing a local division of the Unified Patent Court in Brussels to enhance the protection of intellectual property rights, which is crucial for encouraging research and development among potential innovators.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving intellectual property protection can incentivize R&D investments and innovation by reducing the risks associated with patenting.
reforms.status_evidence: Belgium is considering establishing a local division of the Unified Patent Court.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards improving the legal framework for innovation, which can enhance the overall R&D landscape in Belgium.
reforms.source_quote: Belgium is considering establishing a local division of the Unified Patent Court in Brussels that works in English as well as in the national languages.

reforms.description: The Federal government launched a medium-term action plan 'Digital Belgium' aimed at strengthening ICT usage, developing digital infrastructure, and fostering digital skills and jobs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital infrastructure and skills is expected to improve productivity and innovation across various sectors of the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The Federal government launched a new medium-term action plan 'Digital Belgium'.
reforms.importance_rationale: This action plan represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing Belgium's digital economy, which is critical for future innovation and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: In April 2015, the Federal government launched a new medium-term action plan 'Digital Belgium' to further strengthen ICT usage.

reforms.description: Belgium introduced a new income tax legislation in July 2016 to improve the treatment of income obtained through the sharing economy, establishing a favorable tax regime for individual service providers operating through digital platforms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to formalize income from the sharing economy, potentially increasing tax revenues and encouraging more individuals to participate in this sector, thus enhancing economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was enacted in July 2016.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in tax policy aimed at a growing sector, addressing the unique challenges posed by the sharing economy.
reforms.source_quote: In July 2016, Belgium introduced income tax legislation to improve the treatment of income obtained through the sharing economy.

reforms.description: The Flemish government approved a transition path for Industry 4.0 in March 2017, aimed at enhancing the region's industrial capabilities through digital transformation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to boost productivity and innovation in the manufacturing sector by facilitating the adoption of advanced technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The transition path for Industry 4.0 was approved by the Flemish government in March 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aligns with global trends in industrial modernization and aims to enhance the competitiveness of the Flemish manufacturing sector.
reforms.source_quote: A transition path for Industry 4.0 was approved by the Flemish government in March 2017.
